preview_text:
The model favors Austria at 44.5%, a modest edge over Jordan's 24.5% win probability. A draw is the second-most likely outcome at 30.9%, placing this match in a relatively competitive frame rather than a one-sided affair. Austria's advantage narrows when the full distribution is considered.

The spread across outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty. Austria's win probability exceeds Jordan's by 20 percentage points, but the draw probability is substantial—nearly one-third of all scenarios. This suggests the match is unlikely to be decided by a comfortable margin; competitive, low-scoring play is plausible under the model's base case.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge sharply. Books and Polymarket both price Austria's win probability near 71–73%, roughly 27 percentage points higher than the model's 44.5%. The model also assigns nearly double the draw probability (30.9% versus 17–18% in the market). This gap signals meaningful disagreement about the likely outcome; the dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct, only that they differ substantially.
